metadata.title: Star Platinum in Bizarre Lineage - Sample Stand Page
metadata.description: A sample stand page for Star Platinum in Bizarre Lineage covering quick verdict, investment logic, strengths, weaknesses, and where it fits in the MVP meta structure.

hero.title: Star Platinum: strong sample page, provisional live judgment
hero.description: This page is the MVP blueprint for future stand coverage. It is designed to answer whether a stand is worth the time investment before the reader commits to the grind.

introduce.title: Quick verdict
introduce.description: Star Platinum is a natural sample stand because players instantly understand the fantasy. The page should frame it as a dependable benchmark, while leaving room for balance rechecks.

items.title: Why players care
items.description: It is a recognizable, high-interest stand that makes a strong test case for a searchable database page.

items.title: What this page should decide
items.description: Is Star Platinum worth pursuing for your current goals, or should you move toward another option first?

items.title: What stays provisional
items.description: Exact move values, unlock conditions, and matchup edges should be refreshed whenever the game balance shifts.

items.title: Where it connects
items.description: This page should link back to the tier list for meta context and the beginner guide for players still in early progression.

benefits.title: Strengths and weaknesses structure
benefits.description: A stand page only works if it tells the reader what they gain and what they tolerate in return.

items.title: Strengths
items.description: Frame Star Platinum as a strong all-rounder style sample, especially when a player values straightforward pressure and clean decision-making.

items.title: Weaknesses
items.description: Call out the opportunity cost, matchup limits, or execution demands that stop it from being an automatic answer for every player.

items.title: Best use cases
items.description: Explain whether the stand feels better for general progression, PvP pressure, or targeted goals so the reader can judge fit quickly.

features.title: What a scalable stand template should contain
features.description: These are the durable content blocks that future stand pages can reuse without turning thin or repetitive.

items.title: Hero summary with role and current tier context
items.description: Tell the reader what the stand is for before moving into detail.

items.title: How to get it, only if verified
items.description: Acquisition details should be updated only when they have been checked recently.

items.title: Skill or move overview without made-up precision
items.description: Describe what the kit enables before listing numbers that may already be outdated.

items.title: Strengths, weaknesses, and alternatives
items.description: This is where the page earns trust by helping the reader compare options honestly.

items.title: Internal links to next decisions
items.description: Send the player to the tier list, the beginner guide, or the codes page depending on where they are in progression.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.description: Short answers to common stand-page questions.

items.question: Is Star Platinum the best stand in the game?
items.answer: This page treats it as a strong sample candidate, not an automatic universal best choice. The correct judgment depends on patch state, goals, and what the tier list currently supports.

items.question: Why does this page avoid exact skill numbers?
items.answer: Because the MVP is prioritizing trustworthy structure over unverified precision. Exact values should be added only after a fresh check.

items.question: What should I open after this page?
items.answer: Use the Tier List for broader context or the Beginner Guide if you still need a cleaner path through early progression.

cta.title: Compare before you commit
cta.description: Use the tier list for the big picture, then return to stand pages when you need a more specific investment decision.
